q: Which AWS service provides image and video analysis capabilities using pre-trained machine learning models?
o: Amazon Rekognition which provides pre-trained ML for face detection, object recognition, text extraction, content moderation, and video analysis
Amazon Polly which converts text into lifelike speech audio using neural text-to-speech technology
Amazon Translate which converts text between languages for multilingual application content
Amazon Comprehend which analyzes text documents for sentiment, entities, and key phrases using NLP
e: Correct (A): Rekognition: face detection/comparison, object/scene detection, text in images, content moderation, celebrity recognition, video analysis (people pathing). All pre-trained. B is wrong: Polly generates speech from text - it doesn't analyze images or video content. C is wrong: Translate works with text language conversion - it doesn't process visual media. D is wrong: Comprehend analyzes text (NLP) - it doesn't process images or video.

q: A startup wants to experiment with AWS without paying anything for 12 months. What should they know about the Free Tier?
o: Free Tier provides specific usage limits per service for 12 months (like 750 hours EC2 t2.micro/month), but charges apply immediately if limits are exceeded
Free Tier means all AWS services are completely free with no limits for the first 12 months regardless of usage
Free Tier requires a $1,000 deposit that is refunded after 12 months if usage stays within limits
Free Tier is only available to companies with more than 500 employees and requires enterprise verification
e: Correct (A): 12-Month Free Tier has specific caps (750 hrs t2.micro, 5 GB S3, 750 hrs RDS). Exceed them and charges begin immediately. Monitor with Budgets. B is wrong: Free Tier has strict per-service limits. Exceeding any limit incurs charges. It's NOT unlimited free usage. C is wrong: No deposit required. AWS requires a credit card but only charges if you exceed Free Tier limits. D is wrong: Free Tier is available to anyone with an AWS account - individuals, startups, enterprises. No employee count requirement.

q: What is the shared responsibility model's division for AWS Lambda?
o: AWS manages the runtime environment, patching, scaling, and infrastructure while the customer is responsible for their function code and IAM permissions
The customer must patch the underlying operating system, manage server capacity, and configure auto-scaling for Lambda functions
AWS manages only the physical hardware while the customer manages the OS, runtime, and networking for Lambda
Both AWS and the customer share equal responsibility for patching the Lambda runtime environment
e: Correct (A): Lambda is serverless: AWS manages everything below the function (OS, runtime, patching, scaling, availability). Customer owns: code logic, IAM roles, environment variables. B is wrong: Lambda is fully managed - customers never touch the OS, server capacity, or scaling configuration. That's the entire point of serverless. C is wrong: AWS manages far more than hardware for Lambda - they manage OS, runtime, networking, scaling, patching, and availability. D is wrong: AWS exclusively patches Lambda runtimes. Customers have zero runtime patching responsibility.

q: What is the difference between AWS Budgets and AWS Cost Explorer?
o: Budgets sets spending thresholds and sends alerts when costs approach limits, while Cost Explorer visualizes historical spending patterns and provides forecasts
Budgets automatically reduces your AWS usage when costs are too high while Cost Explorer blocks new resource creation
Budgets is free while Cost Explorer costs $100 per month for any level of access
Budgets only works with EC2 while Cost Explorer only works with S3 storage costs
e: Correct (A): Budgets: set dollar/usage thresholds, get alerts (email/SNS) when approaching or exceeding. Cost Explorer: visualize past spend, filter/group, forecast future. Different purposes. B is wrong: Budgets alerts but doesn't automatically reduce usage (unless you configure Budget Actions). Cost Explorer doesn't block creation. C is wrong: Both have free components. Budgets: first 2 budgets free. Cost Explorer: free to use (API calls charged). D is wrong: Both work across all AWS services, not limited to specific services.
